titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Isaac Backus Papers
titlestmt.author: Staff of Andover Newton Theological School Library and Yale Divinity Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 281 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 5,097 digital files.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 3, 2026 at 18:02

did.abstract: Personal papers of Backus document his professional work and include diaries, travel journals, autobiographical statements, sermons, published and unpublished writings, and extracts from readings. Collected papers include correspondence and narratives chronicling the Separate Baptist struggle for religious freedom, gathered by Backus.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Isaac Backus was born in Norwich, Connecticut, January 9, 1724. Little is known of his childhood. In "An account of the life of Isaac Backus" (completed to 1756), he provides genealogical information and then begins a detailed chronicle of events leading to his religious conversion and his life after that point.

In response to preaching of the Great Awakening, in 1741 Backus joined the Standing church in Norwich. The Norwich minister was distressed that the converts caused too much agitation and after a few years refused to invite itinerant New Lights, so in 1746 Backus and several other church members withdrew and formed a New Light church. In 1748 Backus became minister of a New Light church in Middleborough, Massachusetts; he continued his association with the New Lights until 1756, when he withdrew because of his opposition to infant baptism. He organized a Separate Baptist church in Middleborough and remained as minister there until his death.

Backus published a large number of tracts and a 3-volume history of the Baptist denomination. His two major concerns are reflected in these works: unification of the Separate Baptists and the struggle against religious taxation as part of efforts to achieve religious freedom. Throughout his professional career Backus travelled extensively in New England, helping to organize churches and settle disputes among various Baptist groups. The Warren Baptist Association was established in 1767; Backus was a member of the Grievance Committee formed to work toward elimination of persecution by civil authorities. As an agent of the Committee he worked to keep the issue of separation of church and state before the general public; and in 1774 he travelled to Philadelphia with other Baptists to seek assistance from the First Continental Congress.

In his later years, Backus continued to be a spokesman for Baptists. He argued for ratification of the U.S. Constitution in 1790, convinced that in effect it stood for separation of church and state by prohibiting any religious test for officeholders. In 1796 he published the third volume of his History, and in 1803 an Abridgement. Backus died in Middleborough, November 20, 1806.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in four series:

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the Andover Newton Theological School collection, transferred in 2017

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Isaac Backus Papers, 1660-1806, consist of two major components: personal papers and collected papers. The Personal Papers (1746-1806) of Series I document the professional work of Backus and are arranged according to the following categories: diaries, travel journals, autobiographical statements, ordination booklet, sermons, publications, unpublished manuscripts, and extracts from readings. A card index, prepared by Brown University personnel, provides main entry access to the diary. The diary and index are published: William G. McLaughlin, ed., The Diary of Isaac Backus, 3 vols. (Providence: Brown University Press, 1979)

The Collected Papers (1660-1806) of Series II are primarily correspondence and narratives chronicling the Separate Baptist struggle for religious freedom, gathered by Backus. This section also includes all correspondence of Backus and extant records of the Warren Baptist Association. A card index, prepared by Brown University personnel, provides main entry access to the Collected Papers. The index is divided into three general categories: Baptist History, Letters, Warren Association. The following usage notes may be helpful in working with individual indexes: 1) Baptist History --The scope of the index is self-explanatory. IB references include letters of IB to others and IB as the subject of a specific document. 2) Letters -- The index includes letters of others to IB. 3)Warren Association -- The index includes annual letters of individual churches to the association and correspondence dealing with business related to the annual meeting. The index also provides subject access to the unpublished manuscript, "History of the Warren Association."

The third series includes additional documents, fragile originals, and a set of lantern slides related to Backus. These materials are not described in detail in the finding aid that was received with the collection from Andover Newton Theological School. The lantern slides are stored onsite with restricted oversize material due to their fragile nature. Series IV consists of microfilm of the Backus collection, reels 804-818, which were received from Andover Newton Theological School when the papers were transferred to the Divinity Library. These microfilm reflect the order of the original Backus finding aid.

The original PDF finding aid created by Andover Newton Theological School staff, which contains more detailed description of the collection, is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton and available in hardcopy in Box 19.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Isaac Backus Papers (RG 280), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The booklet has been fully digitized and the records for Massachusetts / Swansea / Second Baptist Church can be viewed using the link in the Massachusetts/Rehoboth/Second Baptist Church file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes extracts on the following: baptism; Cotton Mather; [Charles) Rollin's History; Edes & Gill's Gazette (3/1/1773); Boston Gazette (n.d.); [Joseph] Bellamy; Bishop [Benjamin] Hoadley; Boston Evening Post (5/9/1774); [John] Owen; Life of Hanserd Knollys; Ancient records of Plymouth Colony; Experience Mayhew; [Thomas] Prince; [William] Hubbard's Election Sermon; Massachusetts Colony Law Book (1642); A short account of some of the proceedings of the deputies and committee appointed to take care of the civil affairs of dissenters; [Joseph] Phipps, Remarks on the committee's arguments used to support ecclesiastical impositions in our nation; Quakers; [George] Fox and [John] Burnyeat; Stephen Watt, Essay upon moral agency; Cotton Mather, Magnalia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes extracts on the following : Cotton Mather; Increase Mather; Boston Newsletter (8/27/1761); [John] Gill's Exposition of Revelation; [Isaac] Watt's Logick; Boston Newsletter (4/29/1762); [John H.] Hervey's Letters; Bishop Burnet's translation of History of Persecutions; William Warburton, Sermon preached before the SPG 1766; Massachusetts Gazette (5/28/1767); [Charles] Chauncey, Discourse on the death of Jonathan Mayhew; Hooker, Doubting Christians drawn to Christ; Mayhew, Sermons on striving to be entering at the strait gate; Cotton Mather, Life of John Eliot; Chauncey, Sermon on the validity of Presbyterian ordination; Stoddard, The defects of preachers; Benjamin Church, History of Philip's War; Boston Evening Post (12/26/1768); Ebenezer Frothingham, A key to unlock the door to the religious constitution in Connecticut; Josephus' Antiquities; Benjamin Wallin, Sermon at an ordination in London 1766

did.unittitle: Individual manuscripts: Morgan Edwards, Materials for a history of Baptists in Rhode Island
Individual manuscripts: Paul Bunyan, Prison Meditations
Individual manuscripts: Roger Williams, The fox digged out of his burrows
Individual manuscripts: Letters of Roger Williams and Samuel Hubbard
Individual manuscripts: Diary of John Comer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: fragment: remarks concerning Sarah Thompson statement regarding the Greek word for baptism prayer request of Zulmina Harper and wife; table of contents for Rudiments of North American languages or The young child's grammar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes:J.C., Remarks against infant baptism; [John ComerJ, List of Baptist churches of London ca.1730; A description of Baptist churches and societies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: [John Davis], History of First Baptist Church of Boston (incomplete); Some things relative to the First Baptist Church in Boston; Spiritual autobiography (unknown hand); Elizabeth Lord, Statement of conversion; Anna Plimpton, Statement of conversion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View listing of papers in the original finding aid for the collection, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton, and available in hardcopy in Box 19.

did.unittitle: Backus manuscripts copied by Alvah Hovey in conjunction with research for his work A Memoir of the Life and Times of the Rev. Isaac Backus, A.M.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopied use copies of these letters are filed chronologically in the previous boxes.

userestrict.p: Photocopies should be used instead of fragile originals.

did.unittitle: Labeled folders of photocopies, including: "History of Warren Association" and "Photocopy of Account of Ordination 1745-1805"
Manuscripts of others: "IB Memoir" by Alvah Hovey
Manuscripts of others: "Benson's View of Baptism" by Irah Chase
Manuscripts of others: "Summary of Brown University Theses"
Manuscripts of others: miscellaneous unidentified manuscripts
Genealogy of the King family of Raynham, MA
Pamphlets: "The Mode and Baptism of Subjects Examined in Seven Sermons" by Daniel Merrill, and "The Port Folio", Vol. V. April, 1811, No. 4.
Unidentified notes and "Proposals and Contents of the Baptists Annual Register"
Miscellaneous non-Backus manuscript letters and documents received with collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note in box indicates that many of the manuscript letters relate to the Massachusetts Baptist Missionary Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of slides in box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of microfilm of the Backus collection, reels 804-818, which were received from Andover Newton Theological School when the papers were transferred to the Divinity Library.

did.unittitle: Microfilm of Backus papers, reels 804-818 (15 reels)
Published guide to the microfilm edition (call number BX6495.B32 J64 2003)
